title: Traefik Middleware Use Cases
description:
## Traefik Middleware Use Cases

Traefik middlewares are chainable request/response transformers attached to routers. Each middleware type has a distinct purpose.

A developer wants to remove the `/api` prefix from all requests before they reach the backend service — e.g., `/api/users` should become `/users`. Which Traefik middleware achieves this?

options.text: ReplacePathRegex — replaces the full path using a regular expression
AddPrefix — prepends a path segment to every incoming request
StripPrefix — removes a leading path prefix before forwarding to the backend
RedirectScheme — redirects the request to a different URL scheme

explanation: StripPrefix removes one or more leading path prefixes before the request reaches the backend. For example, with `prefixes: ["/api"]`, a request to `/api/users` is forwarded to the backend as `/users`. AddPrefix does the opposite — it prepends. ReplacePathRegex rewrites the full path via regex. RedirectScheme sends a redirect response (e.g., HTTP → HTTPS) rather than modifying the forwarded path.

hints.title: Listing middlewares in the cluster
hints.body: Middlewares are namespace-scoped CRDs. They must be in the same namespace as the IngressRoute that references them (or referenced with a namespace prefix).
